meta.title: The Clever Monkey — Set 4
meta.subject: English Reading
meta.description: Mixed fill-in and sentence practice for 'The Clever Monkey.'

questions.question: The cats loved _______ more than anything. The monkey used it to trick them.
questions.hint: A yellow food made from milk
questions.explanation: The cats loved 'cheese' more than anything. This is what the monkey used to trick them.

questions.question: What does it mean to 'believe' something?
options: To feel sure that something is true
questions.explanation: 'Believe' means to feel sure that something is true. The cats 'could not believe their good fortune' — they were so surprised!

questions.question: When someone does something 'fairly,' they do it in a way that is equal and just for everyone.
questions.explanation: 'Fairly' means in a fair and equal way. The cats wanted the cheese divided 'fairly' — equally for both of them.

questions.question: The cats needed to _______ the cheese into two equal pieces.
questions.hint: To cut or split into parts
questions.explanation: 'Divide' means to split into parts. The cats needed to divide their cheese so each one got an equal share.

questions.question: Why couldn't the two cats divide the cheese themselves?
options: They did not have hands to hold the cheese
Each cat did not trust the other to divide it fairly
They were too busy eating bananas
The cheese was too hard to cut
questions.explanation: Each cat asked 'How do I know that you will divide it fairly?' — they did not trust each other, so they let the monkey divide it.

questions.question: The monkey looked at the pieces very _______. He checked every detail.
questions.hint: He paid great attention to detail
questions.explanation: 'Carefully' means with great attention. The monkey looked at the cheese carefully before announcing the result.

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'equal' correctly?
options: Both cats got equal pieces of cheese. Each piece was the same size.
The monkey can equal the cheese.
questions.explanation: 'Equal' means the same. 'Both cats got equal pieces' — both pieces were the same size. This is the correct use of 'equal.'

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'The two pieces are not equal.' This is what the monkey kept saying to continue his trick.

questions.question: 'Swung' is the past tense of 'swing.'
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Swung' is the past tense of 'swing.' The monkey 'swung down from the tree' — he swung in the past.

questions.question: The monkey said, 'I am _______ that I ate too much.' But he was not really sorry!
questions.hint: A word that means worried or scared
questions.explanation: The monkey said 'I am afraid' — meaning he was worried or sorry. But he was really just tricking the cats to eat more cheese.

questions.question: Sort each word — is it positive (good feeling) or negative (bad feeling)?
questions.explanation: 'Fortune' (good luck) and 'equal' (fair) are positive. 'Mistake,' 'sadly,' and 'tricky' often have negative meanings in this story.

questions.question: The cats 'thanked' the monkey. What does 'thanked' mean?
options: They were angry at the monkey
They expressed gratitude to the monkey
They gave cheese to the monkey
questions.explanation: 'Thanked' means expressed gratitude — said 'thank you.' The cats thanked the monkey each time he divided the cheese, but the monkey was tricking them!

questions.question: The two pieces were _______the same size — not a single difference!
questions.hint: Perfectly, with no difference at all
questions.explanation: 'Exactly' means perfectly, with no difference. The monkey finally said the pieces were 'exactly the same size' at the very end.

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a sentence about the monkey's trick:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'The monkey ate the cheese little by little.' This means he ate small amounts again and again until it was almost gone.

questions.question: The monkey kept saying 'Uh-oh' because he was really making honest mistakes.
questions.explanation: The monkey was not making real mistakes. He was using 'Uh-oh' as part of his clever trick to keep eating the cats' cheese piece by piece.
